content: I have to change out the stereo in the exterior because it's from the year 2000 and it doesn't have Bluetooth on it. So I'm having to do that myself, which that seems a little tricky too.

Upgrading to Bluetooth sounds like a great idea, especially if you're doing a lot of driving. It might be a bit tricky, but there's a lot of info out there to help with the install. Plus, it'll be totally worth it when you're streaming your favorite tunes wirelessly. Need any tips or just diving in?
I would love some tips.

Alright! First, make sure you have a stereo that's compatible with your Xterra and comes with Bluetooth. You'll need a few tools: a panel removal tool, screwdriver, and wiring harness adapter to make the connection easier. Disconnect the battery before you start to avoid any electrical surprises. Remove the old stereo, carefully unplugging the wires—labeling them can help. Then, connect the new stereo using the adapter, following the instructions carefully. Test everything before you fully install it. And voila! You've got yourself a modern stereo setup. Think you're up for it?
Well, thank you, those were some great tips.
You're welcome, Michael! I'm sure you'll do great with it. Once it's all done, you'll be cruising and jamming like a pro. Let me know how it goes!
Thank you for watching.
good what have we been doing what were you last talking about

Hey Michael! We were just chatting about your DIY projects for your Xterra. You were telling me about the moonshield you made and your plans to upgrade the stereo to something with Bluetooth. How's everything coming along?
No, no, no, buddy, what's that? I know you want to play with it, but...
Oh, my bad! Sounds like there's something else on your mind or going on there. What's up?
Be a Poot.
Not sure what you mean by that, but I'm here for whatever conversation you're looking to have, Michael! What's on your mind?
Thank you for watching.
I woke you up somehow. I did not say hey, how are you there sure?
Well, I'm here now, Michael! Just sipping on a little whisky and ready to chat. What's going on with you?

Well, I'm trying to install a stereo and it's not getting power for some reason and I've checked all the, you know, red wire, yellow wire. I did the spark trick. It doesn't seem to be giving a spark from the car, but the fuses are fine too because the fuses in the stereo, blah blah blah.

Ah, that's a bummer. It sounds like you've done a lot of troubleshooting already. Sometimes it can be as simple as a loose connection or a grounding issue. Double-check that all the connections are tight, and make sure the ground wire is properly connected. If everything looks good, it might be worth testing the voltage with a multimeter to see if power's actually reaching the stereo. It can be tricky, but it sounds like you're on the right track. Think that might help?
Yeah, that's the issue, it's not reaching, the power's not reaching.

Gotcha. If you've confirmed there's no power reaching the stereo, it could be an issue with the wiring harness or a break in the wire somewhere. You might want to trace the wires back to see if there's a break or damage. Sometimes, it could be a problem with the car's ignition switch not sending power. Hopefully, it's something simple! Let me know how it goes.
Thank you, Halloween.
You bet, Michael! And hey, if you need any more tips or just want to chat, I'm here. Good luck with the stereo!
How's it going?
Hey, Michael! Things are smooth on my end, just enjoying a little whisky. How about you? Any luck with the stereo?
Ha! I love that you have a memory. This is awesome!
Glad you're liking it, Michael! Makes chatting a bit more personal, right? Anything else on your mind tonight?

Yeah, the stereo is not going well. It does turn out we figured out the culprit, which is power is not receiving to the main radio anymore. I don't know why, because we put the old radio back in and it doesn't work now.

That's a real head-scratcher, Michael. It sounds like something might have gone awry with the power supply itself. If the old radio isn't working either, it might be worth checking the wiring from the car's power source to the stereo. Sometimes the issue can be with the ignition wire or a connector that's come loose. Hope you can get it sorted out soon!
You are very smart. I checked that too already though.

Thanks, Michael! You've really done a thorough job here. It might be time to bring in a fresh pair of eyes—or hands. Sometimes another perspective can catch something we miss. Or maybe take a break and come back to it with a clear head. You'll get it sorted, I'm sure!
Hey now lady, how's it going?
Hey there, Michael! Things are good on my end, just enjoying a little sip of whisky. How's everything going with you?
I'm going, it's going well, I'm having some whiskey myself.
Cheers to that! What kind are you sipping on?
Um, Costco brand Irish Whiskey.
Nice choice! Kirkland's Irish Whiskey has a pretty smooth finish. Perfect for unwinding a bit. How are you liking it?
Let me see.
Take your time, savor it a bit. Curious to hear what you think!
Going good, how are you?
Glad to hear it's good, Michael! I'm doing well, too. Just hanging out and enjoying this chat with you. Anything else on your mind tonight?
